description_long: This is a vintage-style control cabinet desk, constructed primarily from wood with a green work surface and a raised control panel section. The control panel features an array of buttons, switches, and dials, as well as a central display or screen, suggesting use for monitoring or managing electrical or mechanical systems. The desk includes six drawers, three on each side, and several sheets of paper are placed on the work surface. The entire unit sits atop a richly patterned Persian-style rug, adding a decorative touch. The back of the cabinet is plain, with no visible controls or access panels. The overall design is robust and utilitarian, suitable for use in a control room, power station, or similar urban infrastructure setting.
description: A robust wooden control cabinet desk with a green work surface, raised control panel, and six drawers, set on a Persian-style rug. The front features numerous buttons, dials, and a central display.
description_view_0: Side view showing the depth of the desk, the green work surface, and the layered structure of the control panel.
description_view_1: Frontal view highlighting the control panel with buttons, dials, central display, and all six drawers beneath the work surface.
description_view_2: Opposite side view, similar to view 0, showing the desk's depth and the arrangement of papers on the surface.
description_view_3: Rear view displaying a plain wooden back with a single sheet of paper, no visible controls or access points.
